message:
refactor(package_manager): static list_packages_with_roots, borrowed config path, and project_settings_enabled gate

Three related improvements to the package resolution subsystem:

1. from_override() now accepts Option<&Path> instead of Option<PathBuf>,
   eliminating an unnecessary clone of the config override path. The
   from_env() call site passes .as_deref() to bridge the owned/borrowed
   boundary cleanly.

2. list_packages_with_roots() is now a static method (no &self receiver)
   since it only depends on the ResolveRoots struct and never accesses
   PackageManager instance state. This makes the data flow explicit and
   enables callers to list packages without constructing a full
   PackageManager, which is useful for validation and testing paths.

3. Adds a project_settings_enabled field to ResolveRoots that gates
   whether project-scoped packages are included in the merged package
   list during setup. When false, only global (user-scope) packages are
   collected. This supports scenarios where project settings should be
   read for other purposes (like auto-discovery patterns) but project
   packages should not be loaded — for example, when running in a
   global-only configuration mode.

All affected tests (e2e_cli.rs, package_manager.rs, resource_loader.rs)
are updated to explicitly set project_settings_enabled: true, preserving
existing behavior while making the flag's presence visible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
